Dyna cerddau lle ei mai fel y gall. Mae o meddwl sydd nos wrth gan'r syniad 10, a llymyelim'r rれidiau ar y pros Twe, a ris Robertan yw'r pos Lleitfynnig. Ac mae'n gofynu i'r proffes ac mae'n gofynu i ddweud o'r ffordd yma. Rwy'n gwybod. Mae'n gwybod y bydd y bydd, 90% y bydd, 95% y bydd, mae'r prospecau yn ychydig i'r rhaid o'r rhaid o'r rhaid. Mae'n gwybod, ac mae'n gwybod 100% o'r rhagor, mae'n gwybod i'r brosbeid y bêl. A rwy'n gwybod i'r cyfeirio i'r awr, mae'n gwybod i'r cyfeirio i'r 10, 10, 10, mewn o'r 10 o'r prospecau, o'r 10 o'r cyfeirio i'r company, ac mae'n gwybod i'r 3 10s i ddweud i'r otwr. Ac mae'n gwybod i'r 10, 10, 10, 10, 10, 10, 10, mae'n gwybod i'r 10, 10, 10, 10, 10, If you have it, they're really sure. They love the product. They're really sure about you. And they're really sure about the company that stands behind the product. They have an excellent shot of closing. But what happens is is that it's very difficult to do that in one shot. So now I'm going to draw the straight line out for you, watch. This is your opening right here with the sale begins. This is your close. And your job as an influencer, as a salesperson, is you're trying to push the person down the straight line towards the close. Actually every word you say, everything you do, every question you ask, every document you show, every article, whatever your sales tools are, right? You're trying to move them down the straight line coming open to the close to this ultra high level of certainty, which point they say yes. Right? So that's what the perfect straight line sale looks like. Whatever you say, everything you do, they're right on point. But what typically happens is your prospects try to take you off the line. They want to spiral out. They have questions. They have objections. They talk about things that are not germane to the sale. You want to keep them on the line so you have these healthy boundaries above and below the line. The key to successful selling is to stay inside these values. In control, in control. Add control, add control. Here's where you're spiraling off the Pluto or Uranus. Not good places to be for most of us, at least, right? So your job against someone you have to understand is to understand what looping does, right? So this is your job. Your job is to slowly but surely move the prospect down the straight line. Now, when you ask for the order for the first time, meaning you go through your presentation and you say, here's what I'd like you to do, you know, pick up this XYZ prathe, whatever it might be, and you say, you know, fair enough. Now, where are you on the straight line? Are you here? Are you here? Are you here? The truth is you're actually over here. There's still the beginning of the sale. See, at the point when you ask for the order for the first time, that's when you're ready to roll up your sleeves and make the sale look like this. Does most people, listen, what can they say here? What are the three responses they can say? If you ask for the order, they can say yes, let's do it, which means you have a lay down sale. They say just right on the gate, no objections. Awesome. Very rare. Maybe one side of every hundred sales or maybe twice, a very low percentage. They can say no, meaning not my interests, I don't want it, and that's just as rare, even more rare, by the way, no, because remember, before you even presented, you made your presentation, you went through an intelligence gathering session, and you should have weeded those people out. We'll discuss that in a separate training in the next couple of weeks. Or they can say maybe. So they can say yes, they can say no, very rare, very rare, or maybe. Maybe turns out to be about 97% of the time. What maybe represents is all the common objections. Let me think about it, let me call you back, fair time of the year, let me speak to my wife, my accountant, my lawyer, the butcher, the baker, that you get the whole fucking story, right? All the common objections that drive most salespeople crazy, yet I and all the people who have trained in the straight line, we look forward to those objections because I asked for the order for the first time in my mind that's where the sale truly begins. It's when you can roll up your sleeves and get your chance to earn your paycheck, the real money, for people who said, let me think about it, let me call you back. Now, let me tell me it, right? So now let's really go through this. I want to show you the basic watch. Okay, when someone says, let me think about it, and let's use let me think about it as a catch all for all the common objectives, because they're all the same. All these common objections, you know what they are? They are a smoke screen for uncertainty. That's what objections are smoke screens for uncertainty. And until you get that through your noggin here, you're going to struggle as being a great salesperson because that's all they are because look at it this way. If you presented someone with a product, if they absolutely loved, let's just say they need it because they're in the sale, right? They came to you, you've already been through your qualifying, your prospecting, your intelligence gathering, you know they need your product, they want your product, they can afford your product, so you know that, right? And let's say now, after you've gotten to a 10, and they love your product, they love it, and they also are in a 10 with you, they love you, they trust you, they know you're the shit, right? You're going to give them great customer service, you're going to ask them to the low term, and they also trust your company in a 10. They're at a 10, 10, 10. That's who they are. Let me ask you an honest question. Why would someone say no? Why would someone have to think about that? Let's just think it through for a second. Someone's in a 10, 10, 10. They're not going to say let me think about it. They're not going to say let me call you back. They're not going to say let me speak to my wife unless they really have to speak to their wife, meaning there are some sales where like for instance in real estate, if a husband goes out and buys a home, right? Imagine that. Honey, I just bought a home. He'll get bitch slapped, right? I mean, he's going to get destroyed, right? So of course there are certainly objections can be real, but well, hey, those are different situations. Those are things where you shouldn't be making presentations, at least closing presentations to one decision maker. That's just stupidity. I'm a real estate agent. I don't want to be trying to close the wife when the husband's not their vice versa. It's ridiculous. Okay. So you have to know the industry you're in. But the point is that these objections. Let me think about it. Let me call you back. There's smoke screens for uncertainty because if they really loved your product and if they totally trusted you and they knew the company would stand behind the product, would they say, let me know? They'd say, yeah, let's do it. That's why objections are smoke screens for uncertainty. So given that, given that they're smoke screens for uncertainty, right? What do you do when you get hit with a common objection? Well, first of all, let's let's back up a second. Let's talk about your mindset. This is really important. So one thing that you have to get used to is that when you finish the last word of your presentation, you ask for the order, right? You say, you know, give me a shot. Now I can roll up my sleeves and be a sales person and close the deal. That's got to be your mindset because it will come through in your tonality and your body language and the words you say. How do I close the gap between the two? In other words, I know that if I can get them to a 10, 10, 10 in a single moment in time and ask for the order the right way, I got an excellent shot of closing. I know that history proves that. Then I can close almost anyone who's closeable. If I get into a 10, 10, 10 in a single moment in time and ask for the order the right way, and I'll explain how to ask for the order afterwards. OK? So that's the truth. That's a basic truth. What do I do? Well, what I'm going to do is I'm actually going to go about this in a very scientific systematic way. And I call it picking the lock of their buying combination. In other words, human beings will like, well, it's like a safe. You have a crack the safe, right? And there are all together five numbers in the safe. If you can line up all five of these numbers, you got an excellent, like a 99% chance of closing. You crack all five numbers. So I'm like a safe cracker in a sense. So what does a safe cracker do? When it's safe, let's say there's five numbers, actually there's five numbers in the combination. The first three numbers are number one, they must love your product. That's the first number in the combination. You have to get to a 10. They love your product. Second number in the combination, they must trust and connect with you at a really high level. That's the second 10. Third 10, they must trust and connect with the company. 10, 10, 10. Those are the first three numbers in the buying combination. And I'll get to the last two in about 15, 20 minutes from that when we're done with these three. OK? So that's what I'm doing. So how does a safe cracker go about cracking the safe? We do it one number at a time. And what we do with the straight line is always start to what makes it so beautiful that every sales the same. We always handle it in exactly the same way. So when you watch here. OK? This is your open and that's your close. This is your open. This is your close, right? We move down the straight line to the close. You're at this point in the sale. You have your values here and you have your values here. Right? They say, let me think about it. Right? And when they say, let me think about it, that's your first objection. So all I'm going to do right now is I all I want to find out is where do they stand on the certainty scale for my product? In other words, that's all I want to know right now. I get this other things going on here. But what I need to know before I can do anything intelligent right now to close the sale, I must understand where are they on the certainty? Are they at a one? Are they at a pay or somewhere in between? They're probably not at a one because they wouldn't be on the call anymore. Right? And they're certainly not at a pay at this point because they probably would be a lot more enthusiastic to buy. Right? So what I do is I take this objection. Let me think about it. Right? Let me think about it. Let me think about it. Right? And I engage in a process called deflection. Deflection. What is deflection? Deflection means that I don't answer the objection head on. I deflect it and I take back control of the sale. So watch what happens. It sounds good. Jordan. Let me think about it. Here's how deflection works. I hear what you're saying. But let me ask you a question. Does the idea make sense to you? Do you like the idea? You understand? I hear what you're saying so I don't break the point. I hear what you're saying. My tone out is reasonable. I hear what you're saying. But let me ask you a question. And now the tone out is money aside. Does the idea make sense to you? Do you like the idea? That's deflection. So I took. Let me think about it. And now I'm asking a question. Meaning how do you feel about the first 10? I mean, I should think do you like the idea? Does it make sense to you? Because here's the deal. If they don't like my product, they're not going to buy it. And what I see all the time when I'm watching untrained salespeople is they will try to close someone who's not really that crazy about their product. They gave a sales presentation to someone, right? It means this total presentation. They got hit with an objection. I'm listening because I'm training right this before I start training them, right? So I'm listening to the call. And they're trying to close this person. It's very obvious that the person is still not sure if they even think the product is right to them. And yet the salesperson is still trying to close them. Just think of the sheer fucking lunacy of the bad. You're trying to close someone who doesn't even really like your product that much. What's the point of that? It's a waste of time. So what I do is I take back control of the sale and I say, I hear what you're saying, but let me ask you a question. Does the idea make sense to you? Do you like the idea? Now, obviously, if I'm working, let's say I'm selling TVs or appliances. I'm not going to say to someone, but let me ask you a question. Does the TV make sense to you? Do you like the idea? You can say, what the fuck is wrong with this guy, right? So you have to be elegant here. But I've trained the largest appliance company in Australia, Harvey Norman, right? And I increased their close rate by 30% in one training. So how would you do that? And that's it. So after you gave your presentation, someone said, let me ask you, what do you think of the TV? Is this the right one? What do you think? How does this grab you? Just ask them a question. You don't have to say it makes sense to you. But you say, what do you think of this? You know what? You know what? I understand what you think of the TV. You like that? You like the right one for you? And let them speak. And what they'll say is, yeah, it sounds pretty good. Or yeah, it looks pretty good. What is that on the certainty scale? So I say, do you like the idea they say? Yeah, it sounds pretty good. Is that a 10? This is the magic point right here. This is where the magic happens. They say, yeah, it sounds pretty good. Where is that? It's not a 10. That's like a five or a six. So it says, oh yeah, it sounds pretty good. That's their answer. Where's that? That's it. They say, oh yeah, no, it's awesome. I love this TV. Oh, that's stock. Yeah, it's good. Oh, I love the idea. That's a 10. Or they say, yeah, that's all right. That's a four. Of love. So when I ask that question, does the idea make sense to you? Do you like the idea? The response I get back from the prospect, both the words and even more important, the tonality, show me where they are on the certainty scale. That's the key. That's what I'm doing here. So let's say they say, yeah, it sounds pretty good. That's like a six. Well, is someone going to buy from me when they're at a six? That's just barely certain. You're like men's and men's, right? That's not even close enough to have someone buy from you, right? So what do you do? You have to increase their certainty. So what I do, I say, so what I'll do is watch. I'll say exactly. It really is a great idea. So they say, yeah, it sounds pretty good. I say exactly. It really is a great idea. In fact, the true beauty of the situation. And now watch what I do. This is what why it's called looping. So they, I asked for the order for the first time and they say, yeah, it sounds pretty good. And they said, let me think about it. I say, well, let me ask your question. Does the idea make sense to you? Do you like the idea? And their response is, yeah, it sounds pretty good. That's a six. So I'm going to say exactly. And I use my tonality and enter their world with it. I don't say, yeah, exactly. I'm essentially match their tone out and their enthusiasm. I say exactly. It really is a great idea. In fact, the true beauty is, and then I loop back into the front half of the sale and I make a follow up presentation that picks up where my initial presentation left off. You get that? In other words, the key to overcoming objections, so to speak, well, is to essentially loop around them. So I'm going to hit you with an objection. You know, the smoke screen for these three tens. So what I'm going to do is I'm going to essentially loop back. I'm going to go back into presentation mode, and I'm going to re, re, re present the idea to them a second time. So to do that effectively, I need to have prepared myself before. So before I enter any sale, and this will discuss this in more detail how you do this. We get, we talk another week. We talk about scripting and language patterns, right? But the point is I have already prepared, not just initial presentation. I have my initial presentation, which frames my product and frames the benefits. And now I have a secondary presentation. I've already prepared a tertiary third one I prepared. So I know that when they said, let me think about it. I have two more great language patterns to throw at them to change their mind. In other words, they say they're not that certain. So I'm okay. Let me give you some more information now to make you certain. So essentially I construct my presentation, not to throw every benefit, every feature. I don't kitchen sink them as the phrase goes. I frame why my product is awesome in the front end. I asked the order knowing I'll probably get hit with an objection. And then when I do get it, I loop back into the sale and I hit them with a follow up pattern that picks up right where my initial presentation left off. And this one's even more powerful. It's got, you know, screaming from the whole top logic, right? And it basically, I raise their level of certainty now through the second pattern. So at the end of the second pattern, I'm always in by saying, you see what I'm saying here, really? It makes sense to you now. And I was like, Oh yeah, 99% of the time they will. So by those by looping backwards and giving them a follow up presentation. And then I asked the same question again. Watch, I said, does the idea make sense to you? Do you like the idea? And they said, yeah, it sounds pretty good. And now I looped back. I made a follow up presentation. I asked the same question again. Do you like it? And I didn't say, Oh my God, I love yes, Jordan. Yes. Now I get it. That's the power of your first loop. So now when you ask the question again, they say, Oh yeah, probably a nine now. Now you have a shot of closing them before they were too low on the certain scale. I'm gonna put a little great in my mouth. Give my mouth noise. Awesome. Okay. So you have a great feel. You're taking great yourself. Okay. Everyone getting this. So wall. I had no shot of closing them before because they weren't certain about my product. Now I have an excellent shot of closing was I got them to say, Yeah, no, I like yo, it's great. Yeah, I get it. Yeah, it's a great product. Bing. Now, will I ask for the order at this point? Yes or no? The answer is no, I will not. Why? Because I still have more work to do. There are two more tennis here. That was the first time. Check. Now I have to increase their level of certainty about me, the sales person. So I'll say, so they'll say, Oh yeah, no, I love it. Yes, great job. I'll say exactly. It really is a great idea. Exactly. It's amazing TV. I said, let me ask you another question. Now I go into my next page. I call my forest companion. So if I've been your broker or agent over you and I've been working together for the last three or four years, and I've been making you money on a consistent basis, then you probably wouldn't be saying, Let me think about it right now. You'd be saying, Jordan, pick me up a block of. $5,000. So Jordan, yeah, let's take that insurance policy out right now. Jordan, of course, let's buy this investment property or whatever you're selling. It doesn't matter. But the point is, let me ask you this. You know, if you and I knew each other, if you and I knew each other and I've been selling you things for three or four years and everything I sold you had exceeded your expectations, I essentially under promise over the limit. Your life was better off performing and you probably wouldn't be saying, Let me think about it right now. Am I right? And when you say that to someone in a sales setting, 95% of the time they'll say, Yeah, well, well, then I would because that's the truth. See what you've done now is something incredibly powerful. You've reframed the sale from one of, Let me think about it to I don't really trust you. In other words, if you knew me, if you really knew everything about me, how hard I worked for my clients or the degrees I had, how great I am at helping you make money or saving money or filling a certain need or fixing your pipes or doing your life. You really knew how great how much my clients love me, how awesome I was and how much attention the details. If you knew me, you wouldn't be saying, Let me think about it. Am I right? And they'll say, Oh yeah, well, of course, Jordan, well, then I would. And I say exactly. And that I can understand. You don't know me. I don't have the luxury of a track record. And now I've set myself up to run my forest gum patterns. Now, for those of you who read the book, okay, the forest, you know what this is. Let me just very quickly tell you what the forest gum pattern is. It goes back to the movie forest company, my favorite movies. You can show you all seen it. It's when forest is five or six years old, you're at the bus stop for his first day of school. The bus pulls up, right? In forest is like this. He's doing what forest does. He's standing in space like ours, right? And the bus drivers opens the doors and far as looks at us as gruff look, move a cigarette in the mouth. And he freezes like a deer in the head like doesn't get on the bus. So she says, Well, are you getting on the bus? He goes, My mom told me not to talk to strangers, right? And the woman starts to realize what she's dealing with. So she softens her phone and she says to young forest as well. This is the bus to school. And then far is unmoved by that because it doesn't solve his problem. He doesn't know this person doesn't trust this person. So there's a kind of deadlock there. She was well, are you getting on and finally forest figures out a way to break the deadlock? He goes, Well, my name is forest forest gum. And he goes, Well, I'm Dorothy. I'm your bus driver and it gets on the fucking bus. Now as simple as that example is, it's really fucking profound. See, if you want someone to trust you, what do you do? Tell them that you're trustworthy. Tell them who you are. Tell them your name. They probably forgot your fucking name with this one. You probably just, you know, you probably could be on the phone. They don't know your name, right? Say, let me say now that I can understand. You don't know me. I don't have a track record. Let me take a moment to reintroduce myself. My name is Jordan Belford of John Smith. I'm the managing director of XYZ company. I pride myself on this. I do all this is like to my clients. I go the equity and then what you're going to do in the same way that you wrote a script for your product. You want to write two or three patterns about yourself. Tell them about yourself. Tell them how hard you work that you plan on being the top agent in your company that you have. You know, you work by repeat business. You're not looking to get rich or anyone transaction. You'll be there for them in good times and they will have a come up with ever is growing for your industry and for you. And at the end of that pattern, that my life raises your second 10. That's trusting you. That leaves our third 10. How do we handle that? Very simply at the end of that forest gum pattern, you add on these words and as far as my company goes. I repeat those magic words that easy, right? So after you're done with your forest gum pattern, you go through, you say, why? You say, as far as my company goes. That's it. So you say this. So you end talking about yourself and I'll go the extra mile and as far as my company goes XYZ company, we are one of the most well respected and then you talk about your company. So these are your transition words from your forest gum pattern to now talk about the third 10. It's a my company doesn't want this award. We do be prys on that and tell them about your company. So what you've just done now is you've taken an objection right now. Right now, you've taken an objection. You've taken an objection from the forest gum pattern. You've taken trees. So what? He's your straight line. He's your open. He's your open. He's your close. You're open. He's your close. Your guidelines above and below the line. Okay, so watch you try to go down the line right? They try to go off the line right? You're going to hit the first objection. You loop back into presentation mode where you sold the product. Then you sold yourself. Then you sold the company and now you're going to ask for the order for second time. And now you asked for the order again. That's a loop. So let me repeat here. This is what a loop is. A loop is how you take any of the common objections and use it as an opportunity to build more and more certainty for each of the three tens and then you transition into a close. So it always starts a loop begins by answering someone's objection that you get hit with an objection. You know, okay, it's time to loop right? Your first loop begins with the big D deflection. I hear what you're saying, but let me ask you a question. This is the idea. It makes sense to you. Do you like the idea? That's deflection, right? That's the beginning of your first loop. And from there you're going to resell the product, resell yourself, resell the company and now you're going to ask for the order again a second time. That's what a loop does. And you do that without breaking rapport. And that's the key. See virtually every other objection handling strategy I've ever seen, it's always about getting into a confrontation about why the customer is wrong and you're right, meaning why they should buy and why they shouldn't think about it rather than cutting through all the bullshit and looping back and creating massive certainty for the three tens and then transitioning into a close. That's why it's so powerful. Now, I told you there was three elements. We have about another 10 minutes here to go. 15 minutes, I can go. Good. Any falls on the falls going? Falls going great. I'm asking you to write the Q&A right now. We'll do a Q&A in about seven minutes. There's two more numbers in the buying combination. Let me go through real quick. For those tougher nuts to crack, what you'll find here is that when you actually order a second time here, you'll run into a phenomenon called objection hopping. What is objection hopping? Objection hopping is that let me think about it and say, oh wow, I love it. Can I just call you back? So let me think about it and let me call you back. I should have told my partner or it's not the right time right now, but I call you back. They hopped to an objection. Now, when they do that, the reason typically is either A, they're not quite high enough on the certainty sale. Now, remember there are two types of certainty. There's logical certainty and emotional certainty. So, while you might have got them logically certain, meaning that it makes sense in purity, the features, the benefits, the value proposition, they say, yeah, it makes sense in their gut. They don't have to have it. They can't see themselves using the product and feeling great. That's what emotional certainty is about. You have to create that too. So what we do here is when they, if we run our loop and the loop always ends with us asking for the order of a second time or a third time, right? If you hit the second objection and it'll almost always be a separate one, it'll be a little hop to a new one. The second time now you actually have to answer the objection. You can't keep saying, I hear what you're saying. Then you start to look like you're evasive. So you can deflect the first time and after the first time you have to actually answer the objection. Now, we call those in the sales role of employers. Now I have a book of literally 30 rebuttals for every single objection, 30 rebuttals. Okay. And I'll put a couple of examples out there for you on my, on the, those of you who are actually signing up for this. I'm going to put a couple of examples on there. Okay. But the point is that the rebuttal, all the rebuttal does is give you the right to speak more. Let me explain. Someone says to you, I should kind of call you back and I say, you know, John, I understand you want to call back. And now, now listen closely here. This is the rebuttal. John, I've been doing this for quite some time now. And it is one thing I've seen is that when someone wants to think about it and call you back, they almost never do. Not because they don't love the idea. Because I know you do love the idea. You just go back to your busy life and you'll decide against it. That's a rebuttal. You're abutting their objection about why they don't want to think about it. I have 30 of those and 30 for my wife and every other one. I've created them over the years, right? And I created a book, right? But that rebuttal into itself is fucking worthless. That's not going to close. It's not going to say that and then ask for the order again and close the deal. That's an exercise of utility. What you have to do is you use that rebuttal and that gives you the right to speak more. So when you answer that objection with this rebuttal at the end of the rebuttal, so it will be watched. I'd say, people, you put it in the mind and you'll decide to guess it. And I don't want you to do that. John, let me say this. Let me say I loop back again. And I resell the first 10 again. And the second I'll go right to that process, first 10, second 10, third 10. And I'll start focusing on emotional certainty now and raise their emotional certainty. And then at this point now, when I've hit all three 10s in my loop, rather than asking for the order, I will run a pattern that cuts to the fourth element, which is called action threshold. Your action threshold. This is the fourth of the key elements. The fifth one is called your pain threshold. Watch. I'll show you how they interact, right? I'll say, John, I'll go through the whole thing, right? Well, I'll hit the first 10. I'll hit the second 10. Third 10. I'll run a full loop, right? But before I ask for the order again, I will stop and say, John, let me ask you a question. What's the worst that can possibly happen? Let's say I'm wrong here. And it doesn't matter what product you're selling. You say, and things don't work out the way your product is not everything. And you cash in your guarantee or you lose a couple of bucks or you have to return. It's not going to destroy your life, honestly. And it'll say, no. Say, but on the upside, John, I'm not even half right or half as good as I say. I'm what I could do for you besides this one transaction. First of all, what this will do, this will solve your pain. Do this, you start listing the benefits. Tell them how the product, it's called future paste them. Make, tell them what it's going to feel like when they're using the product, how good they're going to feel, how it results in their pain and your future paste them. So what I did is said, what's the worst thing that happened? Say, not that much, but on the upside, John. So mad for yourself six months and now in your future paste them using your product, getting the benefit, feeling good. Okay. And then the fifth element here would be a job by the way. This is, you could combine this with the paint pressure, which is the fifth element. And when we first spoke, you told me that your biggest headache right now is if you didn't buy this software, your business was basically you're having trouble keeping track of your customers payments.
